```{r, Venn Diagram for 240 vs 144}
                                                                     dplyr::filter(Contrast == "GroupF.Predator.240 - GroupF.Predator.144"))$Module)) + 
  scale_color_brewer(palette = "Paired") + 
  labs(title = "Timepoint 240 vs 144") + 
  scale_x_continuous(expand = expansion(mult = .2))
```

##### Per Timepoint

```{r, Venn Diagram for 48}

VD.DME.48 <-ggVennDiagram::ggVennDiagram(x = list("Male: Predator vs Control" = (WGCNA.DEM.Results %>%
                                                                     dplyr::filter(Contrast == "GroupM.Predator.48 - GroupM.Control.48"))$Module,
                                                 "Female: Predator vs Control" = (WGCNA.DEM.Results %>%
                                                                     dplyr::filter(Contrast == "GroupF.Predator.48 - GroupF.Control.48"))$Module,
                                                 "Predator: Male vs Female" = (WGCNA.DEM.Results %>%
                                                                     dplyr::filter(Contrast == "GroupM.Predator.48 - GroupF.Predator.48"))$Module,
                                                 "Control: Male vs Female" = (WGCNA.DEM.Results %>%
                                                                     dplyr::filter(Contrast == "GroupM.Control.48 - GroupF.Control.48"))$Module)) + 
  scale_color_brewer(palette = "Paired") + 
  labs(title = "Timepoint 48")+ 
  scale_x_continuous(expand = expansion(mult = .2))
```

```{r, Venn Diagram for 98}

VD.DME.98 <-ggVennDiagram::ggVennDiagram(x = list("Male: Predator vs Control" = (WGCNA.DEM.Results %>%
                                                                     dplyr::filter(Contrast == "GroupM.Predator.98 - GroupM.Control.98"))$Module,
                                                 "Female: Predator vs Control" = (WGCNA.DEM.Results %>%
                                                                     dplyr::filter(Contrast == "GroupF.Predator.98 - GroupF.Control.98"))$Module,
                                                 "Predator: Male vs Female" = (WGCNA.DEM.Results %>%
                                                                     dplyr::filter(Contrast == "GroupM.Predator.98 - GroupF.Predator.98"))$Module,
                                                 "Control: Male vs Female" = (WGCNA.DEM.Results %>%
                                                                     dplyr::filter(Contrast == "GroupM.Control.98 - GroupF.Control.98"))$Module)) + 
  scale_color_brewer(palette = "Paired") + 
  labs(title = "Timepoint 98")+ 
  scale_x_continuous(expand = expansion(mult = .2))
```

```{r, Venn Diagram for 144}

VD.DME.144 <-ggVennDiagram::ggVennDiagram(x = list("Male: Predator vs Control" = (WGCNA.DEM.Results %>%
                                                                     dplyr::filter(Contrast == "GroupM.Predator.144 - GroupM.Control.144"))$Module,
                                                 "Female: Predator vs Control" = (WGCNA.DEM.Results %>%
                                                                     dplyr::filter(Contrast == "GroupF.Predator.144 - GroupF.Control.144"))$Module,
                                                 "Predator: Male vs Female" = (WGCNA.DEM.Results %>%
                                                                     dplyr::filter(Contrast == "GroupM.Predator.144 - GroupF.Predator.144"))$Module,
                                                 "Control: Male vs Female" = (WGCNA.DEM.Results %>%
                                                                     dplyr::filter(Contrast == "GroupM.Control.144 - GroupF.Control.144"))$Module)) + 
  scale_color_brewer(palette = "Paired") + 
  labs(title = "Timepoint 144") + 
  scale_x_continuous(expand = expansion(mult = .2))
```

```{r, Venn Diagram for 240}

VD.DME.240 <-ggVennDiagram::ggVennDiagram(x = list("Male: Predator vs Control" = (WGCNA.DEM.Results %>%
                                                                     dplyr::filter(Contrast == "GroupM.Predator.240 - GroupM.Control.240"))$Module,
                                                 "Female: Predator vs Control" = (WGCNA.DEM.Results %>%
                                                                     dplyr::filter(Contrast == "GroupF.Predator.240 - GroupF.Control.240"))$Module,
                                                 "Predator: Male vs Female" = (WGCNA.DEM.Results %>%
                                                                     dplyr::filter(Contrast == "GroupM.Predator.240 - GroupF.Predator.240"))$Module,
                                                 "Control: Male vs Female" = (WGCNA.DEM.Results %>%
                                                                     dplyr::filter(Contrast == "GroupM.Control.240 - GroupF.Control.240"))$Module)) + 
  scale_color_brewer(palette = "Paired") + 
  labs(title = "Timepoint 240")+ 
  scale_x_continuous(expand = expansion(mult = .2))
```
##### Sex Differences Overall

```{r, Venn Diagram for 48}

VD.DME.Sex <-ggVennDiagram::ggVennDiagram(x = list("Timepoint 48" = (WGCNA.DEM.Results %>%
                                                                     dplyr::filter(Contrast == "(GroupM.Predator.48 + GroupM.Control.48 - GroupF.Predator.48 - GroupF.Control.48)/4"))$Module,
                                                 "Timepoint 98" = (WGCNA.DEM.Results %>%
                                                                     dplyr::filter(Contrast == "(GroupM.Predator.98 + GroupM.Control.98 - GroupF.Predator.98 - GroupF.Control.88)/4"))$Module,
                                                 "Timepoint 144" = (WGCNA.DEM.Results %>%
                                                                     dplyr::filter(Contrast == "(GroupM.Predator.144 + GroupM.Control.144 - GroupF.Predator.144 - GroupF.Control.144)/4"))$Module,
                                                 "Timepoint 240" = (WGCNA.DEM.Results %>%
                                                                     dplyr::filter(Contrast == "(GroupM.Predator.240 + GroupM.Control.240 - GroupF.Predator.240 - GroupF.Control.240)/4"))$Module)) + 
  scale_color_brewer(palette = "Paired") + 
  labs(title = "Male vs Female") + 
  scale_x_continuous(expand = expansion(mult = .2))
```



#### Timepoint 48

##### Per Timepoint

```{r, Venn Diagram for 48}

VD.DME.48.48 <-ggVennDiagram::ggVennDiagram(x = list("Male: Predator vs Control" = (WGCNA.DEM.Results.48 %>%
                                                                     dplyr::filter(Contrast == "GroupM.Predator.48 - GroupM.Control.48"))$Module,
                                                 "Female: Predator vs Control" = (WGCNA.DEM.Results.48 %>%
                                                                     dplyr::filter(Contrast == "GroupF.Predator.48 - GroupF.Control.48"))$Module,
                                                 "Predator: Male vs Female" = (WGCNA.DEM.Results.48 %>%
                                                                     dplyr::filter(Contrast == "GroupM.Predator.48 - GroupF.Predator.48"))$Module,
                                                 "Control: Male vs Female" = (WGCNA.DEM.Results.48 %>%
                                                                     dplyr::filter(Contrast == "GroupM.Control.48 - GroupF.Control.48"))$Module)) + 
  scale_color_brewer(palette = "Paired") + 
  labs(title = "Timepoint 48")+ 
  scale_x_continuous(expand = expansion(mult = .2))
```

```{r, Venn Diagram for 98}

VD.DME.98.98 <-ggVennDiagram::ggVennDiagram(x = list("Male: Predator vs Control" = (WGCNA.DEM.Results.98 %>%
                                                                     dplyr::filter(Contrast == "GroupM.Predator.98 - GroupM.Control.98"))$Module,
                                                 "Female: Predator vs Control" = (WGCNA.DEM.Results.98 %>%
                                                                     dplyr::filter(Contrast == "GroupF.Predator.98 - GroupF.Control.98"))$Module,
                                                 "Predator: Male vs Female" = (WGCNA.DEM.Results.98 %>%
                                                                     dplyr::filter(Contrast == "GroupM.Predator.98 - GroupF.Predator.98"))$Module,
                                                 "Control: Male vs Female" = (WGCNA.DEM.Results.98 %>%
                                                                     dplyr::filter(Contrast == "GroupM.Control.98 - GroupF.Control.98"))$Module)) + 
  scale_color_brewer(palette = "Paired") + 
  labs(title = "Timepoint 98")+ 
  scale_x_continuous(expand = expansion(mult = .2))
```

```{r, Venn Diagram for 144}

VD.DME.144.144 <-ggVennDiagram::ggVennDiagram(x = list("Male: Predator vs Control" = (WGCNA.DEM.Results.144 %>%
                                                                     dplyr::filter(Contrast == "GroupM.Predator.144 - GroupM.Control.144"))$Module,
                                                 "Female: Predator vs Control" = (WGCNA.DEM.Results.144 %>%
                                                                     dplyr::filter(Contrast == "GroupF.Predator.144 - GroupF.Control.144"))$Module,
                                                 "Predator: Male vs Female" = (WGCNA.DEM.Results.144 %>%
                                                                     dplyr::filter(Contrast == "GroupM.Predator.144 - GroupF.Predator.144"))$Module,
                                                 "Control: Male vs Female" = (WGCNA.DEM.Results.144 %>%
                                                                     dplyr::filter(Contrast == "GroupM.Control.144 - GroupF.Control.144"))$Module)) + 
  scale_color_brewer(palette = "Paired") + 
  labs(title = "Timepoint 144") + 
  scale_x_continuous(expand = expansion(mult = .2))
```

```{r, Venn Diagram for 240}

VD.DME.240 <-ggVennDiagram::ggVennDiagram(x = list("Male: Predator vs Control" = (WGCNA.DEM.Results.240 %>%
                                                                     dplyr::filter(Contrast == "GroupM.Predator.240 - GroupM.Control.240"))$Module,
                                                 "Female: Predator vs Control" = (WGCNA.DEM.Results.240 %>%
                                                                     dplyr::filter(Contrast == "GroupF.Predator.240 - GroupF.Control.240"))$Module,
                                                 "Predator: Male vs Female" = (WGCNA.DEM.Results.240 %>%
                                                                     dplyr::filter(Contrast == "GroupM.Predator.240 - GroupF.Predator.240"))$Module,
                                                 "Control: Male vs Female" = (WGCNA.DEM.Results.240 %>%
                                                                     dplyr::filter(Contrast == "GroupM.Control.240 - GroupF.Control.240"))$Module)) + 
  scale_color_brewer(palette = "Paired") + 
  labs(title = "Timepoint 240")+ 
  scale_x_continuous(expand = expansion(mult = .2))
```

# Example code for data exploration

Because our data analysis has gotten so complex, there is not really a way that I can provide all the possible combinations of information that you may be interested in. Therefore, I have some sample code that can be edited to narrow down genes of GO terms of interest based on the contrast.

```{r, example code for data exploration}
# For genes

## If you want to look at genes that are significantly differentially expressed in a Males between treatments then you simply filter the DGE.Voom.Results.Info.Table by the contrasts

### If you use DGE.Voom.Results.Info.Table then you will get the same gene multiple times because there is a new row for every gene description or associated GO term. If you can bout the numbers then you should use DGE.Voom.Results.

### To get available contrasts 
unique(DGE.Voom.Results.Info.Table$Contrast)

### Sample single contrast filtering code
Example.Table <- DGE.Voom.Results.Info.Table %>%
  # this gets rid of the description, go_id, and definition_1006
  # if you do not get rid of this then you will have multiple rows per gene do to many-to-one joins between the Gene_Id and the database.
  dplyr::select(1:10) %>%
  # this removes duplicated from the info table
  dplyr::distinct(.) %>%
  # filter
  dplyr::filter(Contrast == "GroupM.Control.98 - GroupM.Control.48")

## If you want to look at genes are are differentially expressed between contrasts that are unique to another contrast (e.g., genes that are differentially expressed between treatment in males but not female), you must filter for both contrast, group by the gene, calculate group size and filter for 1. If you want shared genes then you filter by greater than 1.

### Sample unique between contrasts code
Example.Table <- DGE.Voom.Results.Info.Table %>%
  # this gets rid of the description, go_id, and definition_1006
  # if you do not get rid of this then you will have multiple rows per gene do to many-to-one joins between the Gene_Id and the database.
  dplyr::select(1:10) %>%
  # this removes duplicated from the info table
  dplyr::distinct(.) %>%
  # filter
  dplyr::filter(Contrast == "GroupM.Control.98 - GroupM.Control.48" |
                  Contrast == "GroupF.Control.98 - GroupF.Control.48") %>%
  # group by gene_id
  dplyr::group_by(Gene_Id) %>%
  # calculate the count per group
  dplyr::mutate(Count = n()) %>%
  # ungroup
  dplyr::ungroup(.) %>%
  # filter for unique genes
  dplyr::filter(Count == 1)

### Sample shared between contrasts code
Example.Table <- DGE.Voom.Results.Info.Table %>%
  # this gets rid of the description, go_id, and definition_1006
  # if you do not get rid of this then you will have multiple rows per gene do to many-to-one joins between the Gene_Id and the database.
  dplyr::select(1:10) %>%
  # this removes duplicated from the info table
  dplyr::distinct(.) %>%
  # filter
  dplyr::filter(Contrast == "GroupM.Control.98 - GroupM.Control.48" |
                  Contrast == "GroupF.Control.98 - GroupF.Control.48") %>%
  # group by gene_id
  dplyr::group_by(Gene_Id) %>%
  # calculate the count per group
  dplyr::mutate(Count = n()) %>%
  # ungroup
  dplyr::ungroup(.) %>%
  # filter for genes shared
  # you can increase this as you increase the number of contrasts
  # for example if you have three contrasts then the number should be three to get shared genes between all 3
  dplyr::filter(Count == 2) %>%
  # the next steps are only necessary if you want to count the number of genes.
  # next we just grab the gene_id, gene name, and zfin id
  dplyr::select(Gene_Id, external_gene_name, zfin_id_symbol ) %>%
  # now we get distinct
  dplyr::distinct(.)
  
# For GO terms

## The code to explore the enriched GO terms between contrasts is extremely similar. You just must change the columns. The data structure of these results is slightly different. The GO enrichment results are in a list and nested format for the differential gene expression data and WGCNA modules, respectively.

# if you wanted to look at a single contrast for enrichment using the differentially expressed genes not WGCNA modules
# here you just type $ and the contrast you are interested in and $ result
Example.Table <- GO.DGE$`GroupF.Control.144 - GroupF.Control.98`$result

# Importantly, we look for enrichment within the modules not between contrasts so you would want to do this for modules that you see are differentially expressed in either WGCNA.DEM.Results, WGCNA.DEM.Results.48, WGCNA.DEM.Results.98, WGCNA.DEM.Results.144, WGCNA.DEM.Results.240.

# if you wanted to look at a single module for enrichment using the WGCNA modules
# GO.Modules is the global WGCNA
# here you just type $ and the module you are interested in and $result
Example.Table <- GO.Modules$ME0$result

# if you wanted to look at a single module for enrichment using the WGCNA modules
# Module.GO.Timepoints is the list with modules split by timepoints
# For the timepoint specific modules, you just type $ and the timepoint you are interested in *M48 = 48), $ and the module you are interested in and $result
Example.Table <- Module.GO.Timepoints$M48$ME0$result

```
